For example, they could involve very interesting and simple interventions or investigations that explore new possibilities or challenge existing assump

{"idea": {"Name": "spectral_precision_routing", "Title": "Spectral Precision Routing: Training-Free Geometric Diagnostics for Dynamic Mixed-Precision Inference", "Short Hypothesis": The local spectral geometry of transformer activations (specifically, covariance condition numbers, SVD decay rates, and Hessian spectral proxies) directly predicts numerical sensitivity to precision downscaling. By training-free, online monitoring of these geometric invariants, we can dynamically route tokens through mixed-precision hardware kernels (FP16, FP8, INT8) with zero accuracy degradation, while enforcing strict latency constraints and formal PAC-Bayes risk bounds. This setting is optimal because it bypasses offline calibration or retraining, directly linking mathematical representation stability to system-level latency-accuracy tradeoffs in a way that static quantization or heuristic routing cannot.", "Related Work": Existing mixed-precision inference frameworks (
